title: Agent Control Contract — Metrics Registry (metrics_schema 1.0)
description: DATA catalogue (not a JSON-Schema validator) of the frozen, versioned Prometheus metrics a conformant agent exposes on the opt-in /metrics surface, gated by manifest surfaces.metrics_schema. Metrics are Prometheus 0.0.4 text exposition, not a JSON document, so a per-metric registry of neutral agent_* names is the right artifact for agentctl to codegen dashboards/alerts/scalers from. Versioned additive-within-major (new series / new bounded label values tolerated); removal/rename bumps major. A consumer MUST ignore unknown additive series + unknown bounded-label values and refuse only an unknown major. NOTE: a conformant agent emits ONLY counters and gauges on /metrics — NO histograms — so all histograms here are provisional/unbuilt with bucket boundaries undefined.
x-version-note: 1.1 (additive minor): added the gauge agent_budget_tokens_remaining and the bounded label value limit=tokens_lifetime (RFC 0025 per-instance lifetime budget). A consumer refuses only an unknown MAJOR; these are additive.
exposition: Prometheus 0.0.4 text exposition; /metrics carries NO version label — the version is discovered out-of-band via manifest surfaces.metrics_schema.
cardinality_rule: Bounded labels only. NEVER carry run_id / agent_id / agent_path / call_id / session_id / URI as a label. Closed enum domains each carry an `other` overflow slot. The `server` label is config-time string bounded structurally by a 16-slot intern table (MCP_SERVER_SLOTS); once full, further names fold into server="other".

label_domain_kinds.closed: value is a key of closed_label_domains — a frozen enum ending in `other`
label_domain_kinds.open: free string value (e.g. model) — unbounded by contract, used only where source guarantees low cardinality
label_domain_kinds.open_bounded: config-time string bounded structurally (e.g. server folded to 16 intern slots + `other`)

histograms_note: The duration histograms (agent_run_duration_ms / agent_intel_call_duration_ms / agent_tool_call_duration_ms) are named inconsistently across historical sources (some as otel-only agent_tool_duration_ms / agent_loop_step_duration_ms / gen_ai.client.operation.duration) and no conformant /metrics exposition emits any of them. All histogram entries below are status=provisional with buckets UNDEFINED; consumers MUST treat them as absent unless a future surfaces flag advertises them.
value_type_note: All gauges/counters render as integers EXCEPT agent_saturation, which renders as a float in [0,1] (stored as basis points, divided by 10000). A consumer codegen that assumes a uniform u64 value type breaks on agent_saturation.

metric_record_shape.name: string REQUIRED — neutral agent_* canonical name
metric_record_shape.neutral_name: string REQUIRED — duplicate of name for explicitness (canonical)
metric_record_shape.type: enum REQUIRED — counter | gauge | histogram (frozen 3-way set)
metric_record_shape.labels: Label[] REQUIRED (may be empty []) — each { key, domain }
metric_record_shape.scaling_role: string OPTIONAL — role within the autoscaling subset
metric_record_shape.aliases_scaling: string[] OPTIONAL — cross-source name aliases for the same signal

metrics.name: agent_ready
metrics.help: 1 when ready to accept work (not draining / lame-ducked).
metrics.source: Standard /metrics series (computed from not-draining and not-lame-duck state).
metrics.note: No standalone agent_draining / agent_lame_duck gauges exist; drain and lame-duck state are observable via agent_ready==0 combined with agent_drains_total.

metrics.name: agent_paused
metrics.help: 1 while the agentic tree is paused at turn boundaries. Pause is NOT readiness.
metrics.note: Pause is tracked independently of readiness (agent_ready).

metrics.name: agent_tokens_total
metrics.note: Carries only the {type} label; a {model} label is not part of the contract (deferred).

metrics.name: agent_intel_calls_total
metrics.note: Emitted without a {model} label; a {model} label is deferred and not part of the contract.

metrics.name: agent_limit_exceeded_total
metrics.help: Hard-bound trips by limit (incl. limit=tokens_lifetime for the per-instance budget).

metrics.name: agent_budget_tokens_remaining
metrics.help: Tokens remaining against the per-instance lifetime budget (RFC 0025). Reads 0 when the budget is unbounded AND when it is exhausted — disambiguate via agent_limit_exceeded_total{limit="tokens_lifetime"} / the limit.exceeded event. The alerting/scaling hook for a long-lived reactive instance approaching its cap.

metrics.name: agent_active_subagents
metrics.help: Subagents currently alive in the tree.

metrics.name: agent_tree_breadth
metrics.help: Current max siblings at any tree node.

metrics.name: agent_intel_up
metrics.help: 1 when the intelligence endpoint is reachable.
metrics.note: An older name was agent_intel_endpoint_up; the contract canonicalizes agent_intel_up.

metrics.name: agent_intel_all_down
metrics.help: 1 while all intelligence endpoints are down.
metrics.note: Fleet-routing signal: 1 while EVERY model endpoint is down (the same latch that flips /readyz). Distinct from agent_intel_up (the active endpoint's reachability).

metrics.name: agent_mcp_connect_failures_total
metrics.note: server label is a config-time string bounded by a 16-slot intern table (MCP_SERVER_SLOTS); overflow folds into server="other".

metrics.name: agent_config_reload_total
metrics.note: Config hot-reload outcome counter; result is one of applied / rejected / other.

metrics.name: agent_config_generation
metrics.note: Advances only on a successfully-applied reload (the live generation counter).

metrics.name: agent_pending_events
metrics.help: Reactive events received but not yet routed.
metrics.note: agent_pending_events is the CANONICAL name; agent_reactive_backlog is an ALIAS for the same signal (mechanically a sum).

metrics.name: agent_reaction_lag_ms
metrics.help: Age of the oldest un-routed pending event (ms).

metrics.name: agent_saturation
metrics.note: FLOAT-valued in [0,1] — the only non-integer series; defeats uniform u64 codegen.

metrics.name: agent_claims_lost_total
metrics.help: Work claims lost to another replica.
metrics.scaling_role: over-provision / scale-down signal (high under low backlog ⇒ scale down)

metrics.name: agent_claims_granted_total
metrics.help: Work claims granted to this replica.

metrics.name: agent_claims_released_total
metrics.help: Held claims released back to the fleet.

metrics.name: agent_memory_max_bytes
metrics.note: Emitted only when the kernel exposes the cgroup v2 field; absent otherwise (graceful degradation).

metrics.name: agent_memory_current_bytes
metrics.note: Emitted only when the kernel exposes the cgroup v2 field; absent otherwise.

metrics.name: agent_runs_started_total
metrics.note: Legacy bare series retained additively; prefer agent_runs_total{status} for terminal accounting.

metrics.name: agent_runs_completed_total
metrics.note: Legacy bare series; subsumed by agent_runs_total{status="completed"}.

metrics.name: agent_runs_failed_total
metrics.help: Supervised runs that failed on infra.
metrics.note: Legacy bare series; subsumed by agent_runs_total{status}.

metrics.name: agent_runs_killed_total
metrics.help: Supervised runs torn down by the supervisor.
metrics.note: Legacy bare series.

metrics.name: agent_reactions_total
metrics.note: Legacy bare series retained additively.

metrics.name: agent_tokens_input_total
metrics.help: Input tokens reported by direct children.
metrics.note: Legacy bare series; prefer agent_tokens_total{type="in"}.

metrics.name: agent_tokens_output_total
metrics.help: Output tokens reported by direct children.
metrics.note: Legacy bare series; prefer agent_tokens_total{type="out"}.

metrics.name: agent_restarts_tripped_total
metrics.note: Legacy bare series retained additively.

metrics.name: agent_run_duration_ms
metrics.note: No histograms are emitted; bucket boundaries UNDEFINED. A similar duration exists only in otel. Consumers treat as absent unless surfaces advertises it.

metrics.name: agent_intel_call_duration_ms
metrics.note: No histograms are emitted; buckets UNDEFINED.

metrics.name: agent_tool_call_duration_ms
metrics.source: Declared but not currently emitted; exists only in otel as agent_tool_duration_ms.
metrics.note: No histograms are emitted; buckets UNDEFINED. Name conflicts with the otel-only agent_tool_duration_ms.

metrics.name: agent_mcp_up
metrics.help: PROVISIONAL — 1 when a declared MCP server is connected.
metrics.source: Declared but not currently emitted (no declared-server registration hook).
metrics.note: DIVERGENCE: declared in the contract but not currently emitted. The server label would fold to the same 16-slot bound as agent_mcp_connect_failures_total.

metrics.name: agent_tool_calls_total
metrics.source: Declared but not currently emitted on /metrics (logs/otel only).
metrics.note: DIVERGENCE: declared in the contract but not currently emitted on /metrics.

metrics.name: agent_tokens_per_sec
metrics.note: Autoscaling name not currently emitted; derived/provisional.

metrics.name: agent_intelligence_latency_ms
metrics.note: Autoscaling name not currently emitted; provisional. See agent_intel_call_duration_ms (histogram, also provisional).
